label: The Index Vault
description: The Index Vault serves as the Meridian Lapidary’s storage chamber, sealed behind a thick glass diaphragm cut to the workshop’s rectilinear grid. Shelving takes the form of stacked prismatic cells, each facet-etched with calibration marks that sort blanks, cullet, and finished lenses by angle, density, and resonance response. A ceiling of fixed baffles admits only a narrow meridian band of glare, laying a permanent stripe of light across the aisles while the remainder holds absolute shadow for stable preservation. The floor is dark crystalline strata scored with tensile-line rails that lock crates and racks into immovable, repeatable positions.
